snippet: All overseas students enrolled in Victorian schools can undertake structured workplace learning as part of their learning program. When a structured workplace learning arrangement is being made for an overseas student, the terms and conditions of the visa held by the student must be complied with, as must all the requirements of Ministerial Order 1412. As workplace learning is an important component of a student’s learning program, structured workplace learning undertaken by overseas students does not count towards work entitlement hours of the visa held by the student. For further information, contact the department’s International Education Division on +61 3 7022 1000 or international@education.vic.gov.au Interstate placements Includes requirements relating to interstate placements for ... Chapter in the Structured Workplace Learning Guidelines on requirements relating to overseas students undertaking structured workplace learning Reviewed 24 July 2025
